text.title: JPA / Hibernate Modeling Rules
text.summary: Entity modeling, fetch strategy, equals/hashCode, migration pairing, and read-path optimization rules.

rule.statement: Declare @Table(name = "...") and @Column(nullable = false, length = N) explicitly on every entity field with a constraint.
rule.rationale: Without explicit @Column constraints, Hibernate generates a lenient DDL that diverges from the actual DB schema; the divergence is only caught at runtime.

rule.statement: Add @Version Long version to every entity that may be updated concurrently.
rule.rationale: @Version enables optimistic locking: Hibernate adds WHERE version = N to UPDATE; if another transaction has already updated, a StaleObjectStateException is thrown before committing a conflict.

rule.statement: Implement equals() and hashCode() on a stable business key (email, slug, external ID), never on auto-generated ID, never via Lombok @Data.
rule.rationale: Auto-generated ID is null before persist; using it in hashCode breaks Set membership pre-persist. @Data generates equals/hashCode over all fields including collections, causing infinite recursion.

rule.statement: Pair every entity change with a Flyway or Liquibase migration; never use hbm2ddl.auto=update outside development.
rule.rationale: hbm2ddl.auto=update is destructive in production: it may drop or rename columns unexpectedly. Migrations provide a reviewable, reversible, versioned schema history.

OSIV keeps a database session open for the full HTTP request, enabling lazy loading in views/controllers. This is a footgun: it hides N+1 bugs during development and causes connection pool exhaustion under load.

rule.statement: Default to FetchType.LAZY for all associations; use JOIN FETCH or @EntityGraph at the query level for specific use cases.
rule.rationale: EAGER fetch loads all associations on every query, causing Cartesian product explosions when multiple collections are fetched simultaneously (MultipleBagFetchException).

rule.statement: Use @OneToMany with Set<T> not List<T> to avoid MultipleBagFetchException when fetching multiple collections.
rule.rationale: Hibernate cannot fetch two @OneToMany List collections simultaneously via JOIN FETCH; using Set avoids the Cartesian product issue and is semantically correct for unordered associations.
readOnly = true tells Hibernate to skip dirty-checking on all loaded entities, providing a significant throughput gain on read-heavy paths.

rule.statement: Add @Modifying(clearAutomatically = true, flushAutomatically = true) to all bulk @Query UPDATE/DELETE statements.
rule.rationale: @Modifying updates bypass Hibernate's first-level cache; without clearAutomatically, subsequent finds return stale cached entities instead of the updated values.

text.reference: Repository with derived methods, JOIN FETCH, pageable search, @Modifying
JPA test config with Hibernate statistics and SQL debug logging
